prose:
**Acrolein Fukui Reactivity Functions** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fitted RKS) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1 (neutral reference state); cation and anion single-point energies computed as charge ±1, multiplicity 2 for Fukui derivative evaluation. Geometry: fixed input (acrolein.xyz, 8 atoms: O, 3 × C, 4 × H). Partial charges computed via Mulliken scheme on all three states; Fukui functions derived as f⁺(i) = q_cation(i) − q_neutral(i) and f⁻(i) = q_neutral(i) − q_anion(i). All SCF converged. Charge conservation: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000 ✓, Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000 ✓ (integrity trustworthy = true). **Most electrophilic site:** C4 (terminal olefinic carbon), f⁺ = 0.642. **Most nucleophilic site:** O1 (carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.394. Dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) highlights C4 preference for electrophilic attack (ΔfD = 0.555) and reveals the α,β-unsaturated carbonyl reactivity characteristic of acrolein. Visualization and full atom-resolved tables (fukui_plus, fukui_minus, fukui_zero, dual_descriptor arrays) logged to result JSON and plot file.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
